title: mentha viridis var. lacerata
intro: Mentha viridis var. lacerata, also known as variegated spearmint, is a variant of the spearmint plant characterized by its unique variegated leaves, typically with white or yellow markings on the green foliage. The leaves and stems of this plant contain menthol and other essential oils, which have been used for their medicinal and culinary properties. The medicinal properties of Mentha viridis var. lacerata include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues, reduce nausea, and alleviate headaches. The plant can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and salves, and is often used to aid in digestion, relieve respiratory issues, and promote overall well-being.
